title: cephalotaxus harringtonia f. sphaeralis
intro: Cephalotaxus harringtonia f. sphaeralis, also known as the Japanese plum yew, is a plant species that belongs to the Taxaceae family, characterized by its evergreen foliage and conical shape. The plant has been found to contain taxine, a toxic alkaloid compound that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit cell proliferation and induce apoptosis in cancer cells. The health benefits of Cephalotaxus harringtonia f. sphaeralis include its potential to treat various types of cancer, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and seeds, and are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
